Metric: AI Ethics & Governance Training Completion Rate
Description: Reflects the organisation's investment in training employees on ethical considerations and governance frameworks related to AI.

Metric: AI governance budget as a percentage of total IT spend
Description: Within total AI spending, 2 to 5% of AI budgets are often specifically devoted to governance, compliance, assurance, and responsible AI tools

Metric: AI Security Incident Rate
Description: Tracks the frequency of security incidents related to AI systems.

Metric: AI System Average Uptime
Description: Higher uptime indicates greater reliability and availability of AI systems.

Metric: AI-risk Identification Coverage
Description: Higher coverage suggests a more comprehensive AI-risk assessment process.

Metric: AI-risk Incident Rate
Description: Tracks the frequency of incidents related to AI-risks.

Metric: AI-risk Mitigation Implementation Rate
Description: Tracks the progress of implementing risk mitigation strategies.

Metric: AI-risk Reassessment Frequency
Description: Measures how often AI-risk assessments are reviewed and updated.

Metric: AI-Specific Security Tool Adoption Rate
Description: Higher adoption rate suggests a greater focus on addressing the unique security challenges posed by AI systems.

Metric: Anonymisation/Pseudonymisation/Synthetic Data Usage Rate
Description: Reflects the adoption of techniques to de-identify personal data used in AI systems.

Metric: Audit finding density per AI governance domain
Description: This helps to pinpoint which aspects of your governance framework are most frequently failing.

Metric: Automated Performance Monitoring Coverage
Description: Shows the extent to which AI models are being monitored automatically for performance issues.

Metric: Average decision cycle time vs. Policy-defined time-frames
Description: Lengthy decision cycles can hinder agility and innovation.

Metric: Average MTTR for AI Systems
Description: Measures how quickly AI systems can be restored after a failure.

Metric: Average Residual AI-risk Score
Description: Provides an overall picture of the AI-risk level remaining after mitigation efforts.

Metric: Average time from project initiation to governance approval
Description: Delays in this area can be significant, not only stifle innovation but can also render models obsolete before they are even deployed.

Metric: Bias Detection Test Coverage
Description: Higher coverage suggests a commitment to identifying and mitigating potential bias in AI models.

Metric: Board-level AI risk reporting frequency
Description: Boards should receive updates on AI risk at least annually, quarterly reporting for organisations with substantial AI exposure or critical applications or whenever significant AI projects or deployments occur.

Metric: Compliance with AI Safety Standards
Description: Indicates the extent to which AI systems comply with industry-specific or regulatory safety standards.

Metric: Cost of AI incidents attributable to governance failures vs. technical failures
Description: Understanding the root cause of these costs is crucial for targeted investment.

Metric: Cost of governance delays as a percentage of total project cost
Description: Quantifying the financial impact of governance bottlenecks can be a powerful motivator for process improvement.

Metric: Data Lineage Traceability
Description: Higher percentage reflects stronger data governance and the ability to understand the origin and transformations of data used in AI models, enhancing transparency and accountability.

Metric: Data Minimisation Implementation Rate
Description: Measures the extent to which data minimisation practices are being applied to AI systems.

Metric: Dataset Bias Audit Rate
Description: Reflects proactive efforts to ensure training data does not perpetuate or amplify existing biases.

Metric: Documentation Completeness
Description: Measures the extent to which AI development, deployment, and monitoring processes are documented, contributing to transparency and auditability.

Metric: Documentation completeness score at each stage
Description: Inadequate documentation is a recurring theme, complicating bias detection, error correction, and audits.

Metric: Estimated compliance cost as a percentage of the AI program budget
Description: This provides a clear financial picture of the cost of compliance.

Metric: Estimated time and cost to achieve compliance with new regulations
Description: Will vary between sectors & AI use-cases.

Metric: Fairness Metric Tracking Rate
Description: Shows the extent to which fairness metrics are being used to measure and monitor AI models for potential bias.

Metric: Governance coverage percentage by AI use case type
Description: This highlights which areas of AI application have the most robust governance.

Metric: Internal AI Governance Audit Frequency
Description: Measures how often the organisation conducts internal audits to assess its AI governance practices.

Metric: Mean time to AI incident detection
Description: The goal is to continuously reduce this window of vulnerability.

Metric: Number of AI projects that would need to be paused or modified
Description: This assesses the potential disruption to the innovation pipeline.

Metric: Number of current processes requiring modification
Description: This highlights the scale of the adaptation challenge.

Metric: Number of dedicated AI governance FTEs
Description: Organisations handling high-risk AI (e.g., pharma, financial services) often employ more governance resources relative to revenue than those focused on low-risk, internal tools.

Metric: Number of escalation paths that bypass designated decision-makers
Description: Frequent bypassing of formal channels indicates a flawed or inefficient process.

Metric: Number of governance checkpoints bypassed per project
Description: This metric directly reflects the adherence to, or disregard for, established AI governance protocols.

Metric: Number of governance processes that failed during AI incident response
Description: This analysis helps to identify the weakest links in the governance chain.

Metric: Number of post-deployment governance issues discovered
Description: A high number of issues post-launch often points to weaknesses in the initial governance and review process.

Metric: Number of process changes implemented post-incident
Description: The true test of learning is whether insights from an incident lead to sustained process improvements.

Metric: Number of vendor contracts with AI-specific governance clauses
Description: Contracts should explicitly address data rights, model transparency, and liability.

Metric: Percentage of AI incidents revealing unknown governance gaps
Description: A significant percentage here indicates a reactive rather than proactive governance posture.

Metric: Percentage of AI projects with formal risk assessments
Description: Auditing AI models will fast become a non-negotiable requirement with large organisations facing more scrutiny in disparate jurisdictions.

Metric: Percentage of AI vendors with completed due diligence assessments
Description: This is a fundamental first step in managing vendor risk.

Metric: Percentage of employees who can correctly identify their AI governance responsibilities
Description: A lack of clarity at the executive level can lead to inconsistent and ineffective governance (tricky, but powerful)

Metric: Percentage of staff requiring additional training
Description: A prepared workforce is central to successful regulatory adaptation.

Metric: Percentage of vendor AI changes that trigger internal governance reviews
Description: Changes to vendor models can introduce new risks that require attention.

Metric: Policy compliance scores across different business units
Description: This can identify departments or teams that may require additional training or support.

Metric: Risk assessment completion rates by model complexity
Description: This can reveal if more complex, and potentially riskier, projects are receiving the necessary scrutiny.

Metric: Stress Test Pass Rate
Description: Reflects the resilience of AI systems under high workloads or unexpected inputs.

Metric: Time to Resolve Data Breaches Involving AI
Description: Shorter resolution times indicate a more efficient and effective response to data breaches.

Metric: Vendor incident notification compliance rate
Description: Timely notification of incidents is critical for effective response.
